dailyDharma.heading: Begin Your Journey to Inner Peace
dailyDharma.subHeading: Discover daily practices crafted to nurture your mind and spirit.
dailyDharma.findPeaceTitle: Find Peace and Clarity with Daily Practices
dailyDharma.findPeaceSubtitle1: Simple routines to support your well-being, tailored for you.
dailyDharma.findPeaceSubtitle2: Make dharma a part of your daily rhythm. This space offers bite-sized practices — chants, affirmations, reflections, and mindful rituals — designed to ground you in inner peace and purpose. Start your day with spiritual clarity and return whenever your soul needs a reset.
dailyDharma.yourPathSubtitle: Take the first step toward a calmer, healthier life.
dailyDharma.step1Subtitle: Select practices that align with your spiritual goals, based on your experience level.
dailyDharma.step2Subtitle: Pick 1–3 daily rituals to build a simple practice that fits your life.
dailyDharma.step3Subtitle: Stay consistent and reflect on your growth through gentle daily tracking.
dailyDharma.signInTitle: Sign in to KalpX to personalize your daily practice.
dailyDharma.quote: “With a pure heart, every moment becomes a sacred practice.”
dailyDharma.faqDescription: KalpX Daily Practice. Show up every day — and watch the shift begin.
dailyDharma.familySubtitle1: Come together in devotion before meals, growing in spirit as a family.
dailyDharma.familySubtitle2: Strengthen family bonds through shared rituals that connect you to Sanatan Dharma, fostering love, gratitude, and cultural heritage in every meal.
dailyDharma.familyFooter: Create a shared journey for peace and connection!
dailyDharma.faq1Title: Why build a daily spiritual practice?
dailyDharma.faq1Desc:
In a world of constant noise, daily spiritual practice is a quiet anchor. It grounds your energy, purifies your intent, and gently brings you back to what matters. KalpX offers simple, accessible practices — from morning chants and breathwork to evening reflection and gratitude — so that spirituality becomes not a task, but a rhythm.

Whether you light a diya before work, repeat a mantra on your commute, or take 2 minutes to sit in silence before sleep — these small rituals create big shifts over time. Consistency becomes connection. Repetition becomes realization.

Let KalpX help you infuse your everyday with purpose, presence, and peace.
dailyDharma.faq2Title: What you’ll find in Daily Practice
dailyDharma.faq2Desc:
✔ Morning invocations, chants, and affirmations to start the day
✔ Guided meditations for clarity, energy, and calm
✔ Breathwork and grounding techniques based in yogic science
✔ Short reflections on dharma, gratitude, and intention-setting
✔ Evening wind-down rituals and sacred closing prayers
✔ Bite-sized stories and teachings for daily inspiration
✔ Practices designed for all ages, including children and elders
dailyDharma.faq3Desc:
✔ Anyone looking to cultivate inner calm and consistency
✔ Beginners unsure where to start with spiritual habits
✔ Parents wanting simple rituals to share with their kids
✔ Professionals needing short, grounding pauses in their day
✔ Seekers seeking structure without pressure
✔ Elders returning to daily devotion
dailyDharma.faq4Desc:
🪔 Choose one simple practice to begin with — and stay consistent
⏰ Set a regular time (sunrise, lunch break, before bed)
📿 Use malas, bells, or incense to engage the senses
📱 Bookmark your favorite KalpX routines for easy access
👨‍👩‍👧 Create a shared family ritual to build cultural memory
dailyDharma.faq5Title: What makes KalpX Daily Practice different?
dailyDharma.faq5Desc:
✔ Spiritually potent, yet simple and practical
✔ Crafted for modern life — short formats, flexible timing
✔ Deeply rooted in Sanatan Dharma’s daily rhythm
✔ Accessible to all — no prior knowledge required
✔ A blend of devotion, discipline, and discovery

kalpx.pathDesc: Explore Classes in dance, music, mantras, yoga and scripture- at your pace
kalpx.visualWisdomDesc: Watch guided videos on culture, wisdom, rituals, and spiritual practices—anytime, anywhere
kalpx.sacredRitualsDesc: Discover meaningful rituals, daily practices, and devotional routines to deepen your spiritual journey

testimonials.author: Rahul Mehta
testimonials.text: I like how KalpX focuses on daily life, not just theory. The Sankalp helps me stay mindful throughout the day.

testimonials.author: Sandeep Patel
testimonials.text: The daily sankalp practices bring a gentle structure to my routine. It doesn't feel heavy, just gently guiding.

testimonials.author: Ananya Sharma
testimonials.text: Beautiful interface and even more beautiful content. The wisdom videos are a great way to start my morning.

testimonials.author: Vikram Singh
testimonials.text: I've tried many meditation apps, but KalpX's focus on Sanatan Dharma makes it unique and deeply meaningful.

testimonials.author: Priya Iyer
testimonials.text: The community features help me stay connected with like-minded people. It's a very supportive environment.

explore.title: Explore Videos
explore.subtitle: Reconnect with Your Roots — Start Watching

faq.title: Why Explore Spiritual Videos?
faq.description: In a fast-moving world, we often forget to pause and reflect. KalpX Videos offer a moment to reconnect — with timeless wisdom, inspiring stories, sacred movement, and devotional depth — wherever you are. Whether you’re watching a classical Bharatanatyam performance, a sacred mantra chant, a ritual from a temple, or a short teaching on karma or consciousness, each video opens a window into Sanatan Dharma’s living heartbeat. You’ll also find guided meditations, yoga practices, spiritual music, and cultural storytelling — all curated to help you grow in awareness, devotion, and joy. Let these visuals transport you beyond the screen — into a space of reverence, inquiry, and inner clarity. Watch alone, with family, or as part of your daily spiritual rhythm.

faq.title: What You’ll Find in Explore Videos
faq.description:
• ✔ Teachings on dharma, consciousness, and inner peace
• ✔ Rituals and poojas from sacred temples across Bharat
• ✔ Classical Indian dance (Bharatanatyam, Odissi, Kathak & more)
• ✔ Guided yoga, meditation, and breathwork rooted in tradition
• ✔ Devotional music, bhajans, kirtans, and mantra chanting
• ✔ Stories from the Ramayana, Mahabharata, and Puranas
• ✔ Culturally rich videos for children and families
• ✔ Dialogues with gurus, yogis, artists, and wisdom keepers

faq.title: Who Are Curated KalpX Videos For?
faq.description:
• ✔ Spiritual seekers and lifelong learners
• ✔ Diaspora audiences reconnecting with their heritage
• ✔ Artists, dancers, yogis, and musicians honoring sacred traditions
• ✔ Parents introducing dharma to children in engaging ways
• ✔ Anyone looking for screen time that uplifts, educates, and transforms

faq.title: What Makes Curated KalpX Videos Different?
faq.description:
• ✔ Authentic, lineage-rooted content curated with care
• ✔ Visuals that inspire devotion, stillness, and self-inquiry
• ✔ Diverse voices, regional traditions, and cultural depth
• ✔ Made for transformation — not just passive viewing

faq.title: How Can I Find Videos That Interest Me?
faq.description:
• ✔ Use the search bar to find specific topics.
• ✔ Filter by category, language, or tags to narrow down your selection.
• ✔ Toggle the Kids Hub for family-friendly content.

faq.title: Are the Videos Available in Multiple Languages?
faq.description:
• ✔ Yes, videos are available in languages like English, Hindi, Tamil, Telugu, and more.
• ✔ Select your preferred language from the filters to view relevant content.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: KalpX ("we", "our", or "us") operates the KalpX Mitra companion app. This Privacy Policy explains what personal information we collect, how we use it, and your rights over it. Privacy contact: privacy@kalpx.com.
privacy.section1Content: Account and profile data (name, email, password hash, preferences). Timezone for notification timing. Push notification tokens. Notification delivery receipts. Companion memory and personalization signals (CompanionState — structured summary, not conversation transcripts). Tell Mitra messages stored encrypted. Voice note audio (deleted within 24 hours). Voice note transcripts (retained while account is active). Activity data (rhythm, inner path, rooms, check-ins).
privacy.section2Content: We use analytics tools to understand product usage and improve the experience. We do not send raw Tell Mitra messages, voice transcripts, or emotional state labels to advertising platforms.
privacy.section3Content: To deliver companion guidance, send timely notifications, personalize Mitra responses, process Tell Mitra interactions, transcribe voice notes and delete raw audio promptly, measure product performance, and comply with legal obligations.
privacy.section4Content: Account and journey data: retained while active; deleted with account. Voice audio: deleted within 24 hours. Voice transcripts: retained while active. Notification delivery receipts: approximately 12 months. Server logs: approximately 90 days. Backups: may persist 30–90 days.
privacy.section5Content: Tell Mitra messages are encrypted at rest. We use HTTPS/TLS for data in transit and hashed password storage.
privacy.section6Content: You may access, update, or delete your data. Contact privacy@kalpx.com. You can withdraw push notification consent in Settings. You can withdraw voice consent in app settings.
privacy.section7Content: To delete your account: use Profile → Delete Account in the app, or email privacy@kalpx.com with subject "Delete My KalpX Account". We generally process verified deletion requests within 30 days. Backup copies may persist 30–90 days. Some data may remain in anonymized or aggregated form.
privacy.section8Content:
Privacy contact: privacy@kalpx.com
KalpX, Inc., Delaware, USA

section1.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
section1.text: By accessing and using KalpX, you agree to abide by our specific Terms of Service and Community Rules. Participation in this platform implies a commitment to the Dharmic values we uphold.

section2.title: 2. Content Ownership & License
section2.text: You retain ownership of the content you post on KalpX. However, by posting, you grant KalpX a non-exclusive, royalty-free license to display, distribute, and promote your content within the platform and its related services.

section3.title: 3. Moderation & Adharmic Content
section3.text: KalpX reserves the right to review and remove any content that violates our Community Rules or is deemed Adharmic (against the principles of righteousness and truth). We strive to maintain a pure and positive environment.

section4.title: 4. Termination
section4.text: We reserve the right to suspend or terminate accounts that repeatedly violate these agreements or engage in harmful behavior. This is to protect the sanctity of the community.

section5.title: 5. Changes to Agreements
section5.text: These agreements may be updated to reflect the evolving needs of our growing spiritual community. Continued use of the platform after changes implies acceptance of the new terms.

post.linkedMantraGeneral: Do this mantra today - let intention become action

rules.title: KalpX Community Rules

rule1.title: 1. Respect for Dharma (Righteousness)
rule1.text: We uphold truth, compassion, and non-violence (Ahimsa) in all our interactions. Speak with kindness and integrity. Adhere to the principles of Dharma in your conduct within the community.

rule2.title: 2. Authenticity of Scripture
rule2.text: When discussing Shastras, Vedas, Puranas, or other sacred texts, please cite your sources respectfully and accurately. Misleading interpretations or fabricating information is discouraged.

rule3.title: 3. No Hate Speech or Adharmic Conduct
rule3.text: We have zero tolerance for malice, blasphemy, discrimination, or intent to harm others. Any speech that spreads hatred or division is considered Adharmic and will be removed.

rule4.title: 4. Constructive Debate (Shastrartha)
rule4.text: Debates should be grounded in the spirit of Jigyasa (seeking truth), not Ahankara (ego). Engage in discussions to learn and share knowledge, not to prove superiority.

rule5.title: 5. Respect for Deities and Gurus
rule5.text: Maintain reverence when speaking of Deities, Gurus, and spiritual teachers. Constructive discussion is welcome, but disrespect is not tolerated.

rules.quoteTranslation: Dharma protects those who protect it.

section1.title: 1. Sanctity of Your Data
section1.text: We consider your personal sadhana data, journal entries, and spiritual progress as sacred and private. We treat your information with the highest level of confidentiality and respect.

section2.title: 2. Data Collection
section2.text: We collect minimal data necessary to provide you with a personalized spiritual experience. This includes your username, email (for account recovery), and your interactions within the community to improve content recommendations.

section3.title: 3. Usage of Information
section3.text: Your data is used solely to enhance your journey on KalpX. We do not sell your personal information to third-party advertisers. We believe your spiritual growth is not a product.

section4.title: 4. Third-Party Services
section4.text: We may use trusted third-party services for hosting, analytics, and communication. These partners are bound by strict confidentiality agreements and are only provided with data necessary to perform their functions.

section5.title: 5. Security
section5.text: We employ industry-standard security measures to protect your data from unauthorized access, alteration, or disclosure. However, no method of transmission over the internet is 100% secure.

about.heroHeading: The heart of your inner journey
about.heroText: KalpX is home to countless paths for personal growth, mindful practices, and genuine self-discovery. Whether you’re exploring meditation, building inner resilience, seeking clarity in your career, or simply nurturing calm in daily life, there’s a guided journey on KalpX for you.
about.postText: The Community can share content by posting stories, links, image, and Videos.
about.commentText: The Community can share content by posting stories, links, images, and videos.
about.commentBubble1: I Like the fact that everyone is here is so spiritual
about.commentBubble2: Pure devotion, pure strength. This story never fails to touch the heart.
about.voteText: Components & posts can be Upvoted or downvoted. The most interesting content rises to the top.

turn8.footer: This isn't homework. It's sadhana — a daily practice that builds something real over time.

mitraStart.title:
I'm Mitra.
I'm here with you.
mitraStart.line2: I am here to help you feel more calm, steady, and clear — on hard days and good days.
mitraStart.line3: I notice small things, like your mood and the shape of your day.

connection_named.title: Name someone who matters
connection_named.sanatanContext: Sambandha reminds us that even one true bond can hold us.
connection_named.whyWeAsk: Naming someone helps you return from feeling alone to one thread of care.
connection_named.prompt: Who is close to your heart right now?
connection_named.placeholder: Write a name, relationship, or a few words…

joy_named.title: Write what's good right now
joy_named.sanatanContext: Santosha begins by noticing what is already enough.
joy_named.whyWeAsk: Writing one good thing helps the mind stay with it instead of rushing past it.
joy_named.prompt: What feels good, steady, or quietly enough right now?

growth_journal.title: Write what you noticed
growth_journal.sanatanContext: Growth ripens through one right action, not speed.
growth_journal.whyWeAsk: Naming what you noticed turns observation into the seed of a next step.
growth_journal.prompt: What did you notice, or what is forming?

connection_reach_out.title: Reach out to one person
connection_reach_out.sanatanContext: A short act of reaching is itself the practice of sambandha.
connection_reach_out.whyWeAsk: Writing the message, even without sending, brings the connection closer.
connection_reach_out.prompt: Write a short message to someone who matters.

release_named.title: Name what you're setting down
release_named.sanatanContext: Letting go is not giving up. It is loosening the grip so life can move again.
release_named.whyWeAsk: Naming the weight helps you separate yourself from what you are carrying.
release_named.prompt: What is ready to be set down for now?
release_named.placeholder: Write one word or a few lines…

stillness_named.title: Write what became still
stillness_named.sanatanContext: Stillness begins when attention returns to one steady anchor.
stillness_named.whyWeAsk: Naming what settled helps you recognize the ground beneath the noise.
stillness_named.placeholder: Write one word or a few lines…

clarity_journal.title: Write one honest question
clarity_journal.sanatanContext: Clarity comes when we stop obeying confusion and look at what is actually here.
clarity_journal.whyWeAsk: Writing the question separates the real decision from the noise around it.
clarity_journal.prompt: What is the question you are actually sitting with?
